lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
Epiphanius, in order to gratify Theophilus, performs Ordinations at Constantinople without John's Permission.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Epiphanius, in order to gratify Theophilus, performs Ordinations at Constantinople without John's Permission.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Not long after this, at the suggestion of Theophilus, the bishop Epiphanius again came from Cyprus to Constantinople; he brought also with him a copy of the synodical decree in which they did not excommunicate Origen himself but condemned his books.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: On reaching St. John’s church, which is seven miles distant from the city, he disembarked, and there celebrated a service; then after having ordained a deacon, he again entered the city.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: In complaisance to Theophilus he declined John’s courtesy, and engaged apartments in a private house.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He afterwards assembled those of the bishops who were then in the capital, and producing his copy of the synodical decree condemnatory of Origen’s works, recited it before them; not being able to assign any reason for this judgment, than that it seemed fit to Theophilus and himself to reject them.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Some indeed from a reverential respect for Epiphanius subscribed the decree; but many refused to do so among whom was Theotimus bishop of Scythia, who thus addressed Epiphanius:—‘I neither choose, Epiphanius,’ said he, ‘to insult the memory of one who ended his life piously long ago; nor dare I be guilty of so impious an act, as that of condemning what our predecessors did not reject: and especially when I know of no evil doctrine contained in Origen’s books.’

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Having said this, he brought forward one of that author’s works, and reading a few passages therefrom, showed that the sentiments propounded were in perfect accordance with the orthodox faith.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He then added, ‘Those who speak evil of these writings are unconsciously casting dishonor upon the sacred volume whence their principles are drawn.’

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Such was the reply which Theotimus, a bishop celebrated for his piety and rectitude of life, made to Epiphanius.
